q.en: Why did the people on Earth need someone to tip the moon bowl?
q.zh: 地球上的人们为什么需要有人去打翻月亮碗？

options.en: All rivers ran into deep holes and there were no ponds or lakes to save drinking water
options.zh: 所有的河流都流进了地下的深洞，没有池塘或湖泊来储存饮用水

options.en: They wanted to flood the king's castle
options.zh: 他们想淹没国王的城堡

options.en: The princess asked for a swimming pool
They needed water to wash their clothes only

explain.en: The book says: 'all the rivers in the world ran into deep holes in the ground. There were no ponds or lakes where people could save their drinking water.' The bowl tipped = rain.
explain.zh: 书中说：“世界上所有的河流都流进了地下的深洞。没有池塘或湖泊让人们储存饮用水。”碗被打翻就等于下雨。

q.en: In the glossary, what does the word 'humble' mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，“humble”一词是什么意思？

options.en: Modest; a common person, not royalty
options.zh: 谦逊的；普通人，不是 royalty（贵族）

options.en: A king or queen
Shining softly
Weak and shaky

explain.en: The glossary defines humble (adj.) as 'modest; a common person, not royalty (p. 6).' Tom is called 'a humble woodchopper.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 humble（形容词）定义为“谦逊的；普通人，不是贵族（第6页）”。汤姆被称作“一个谦逊的樵夫”。

q.en: Who is the hero of this fairy tale?
q.zh: 这个童话故事的主角是谁？

options.en: The king
A humble woodchopper named Tom
options.zh: 一个叫汤姆（Tom）的谦逊樵夫

options.en: The old man on the moon
Princess Charlotte's father

explain.en: The book says: 'The hero was a humble woodchopper named Tom.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“主角是一个叫汤姆的谦逊樵夫。”

q.en: What task does the king give Tom before he can marry Princess Charlotte?
q.zh: 在汤姆能迎娶夏洛特公主之前，国王给了他什么任务？

options.en: Chop down the largest tree in the forest
Go to the woman on the moon and make her tip the moon bowl so they get rain
options.zh: 去月亮上找那个女人，让她打翻月亮碗，这样他们就能下雨

options.en: Organize the stars in the sky
Build a new castle

explain.en: The king says: 'He must go to that silly woman on the moon. He must make her tip the moon bowl over so we might have some rain.'
explain.zh: 国王说：“他必须去月亮上找那个傻女人。他必须让她打翻月亮碗，这样我们才能下点雨。”

q.en: How does Tom travel all the way to the moon?
q.zh: 汤姆是怎样一路到达月亮上的？

options.en: He builds a rocket
He closes his eyes and, on the third chop with his axe, flies straight to the moon
options.zh: 他闭上眼睛，用斧头砍到第三下时，直接飞到了月亮上

options.en: He rides a horse
The princess carries him

explain.en: The book says Tom closed his eyes, and 'on the third chop that Tom chopped with his eyes closed, whiz-bang, he flew straight to the moon.'
explain.zh: 书中说汤姆闭上眼睛，“在汤姆闭着眼睛砍的第三下，嗖地一下，他直接飞到了月亮上。”

q.en: Whom does Tom meet on the moon, and what is that person doing?
q.zh: 汤姆在月亮上遇到了谁？那个人在做什么？

options.en: A rickety old man drawing a diagram of how to line up the stars with a glass stick
options.zh: 一个摇摇晃晃的老人，正用一根玻璃棒画如何排列星星的示意图

options.en: The princess Charlotte
A giant cooking soup
The king of the moon

explain.en: Tom meets 'an old man stooping over the ground... using the stick to draw in the dust' a 'diagram of how to line up the stars.'
explain.zh: 汤姆遇到“一个弯腰伏在地上的老人……用棒子在尘土上画画”，画的是“如何排列星星的示意图”。

q.en: What does the old man believe is his truly important work?
q.zh: 老人认为自己真正重要的工作是什么？

options.en: Tipping the moon bowl every time it is full
options.zh: 每次碗满了就打翻月亮碗

options.en: Lining up and organizing the stars
Chopping wood like Tom
Washing the king's clothes

explain.en: The old man says: 'My work is much too important for me to waste time... I'm dealing with the stars, boy, and the stars are very important.' He thinks lining up stars is his real work.
explain.zh: 老人说：“我的工作太重要了，不能浪费时间……我在处理星星，孩子，星星非常重要。”他认为排列星星才是自己真正的工作。

q.en: How does Tom finally make the old man do his job of tipping the bowl?
q.zh: 汤姆最终是如何让老人去履行打翻碗的职责的？

options.en: He fights the old man
The old man fell into the bowl; Tom took the glass stick and left, so the old man had to tip it to avoid the water
options.zh: 老人掉进了碗里；汤姆拿走了玻璃棒并离开，老人为了不淹着只好打翻碗

options.en: He asked the princess for help
He tipped it himself

explain.en: The old man falls into the bowl; Tom says 'now that you're inside the bowl, I suppose you'll do your job... If you don't tip the bowl when it's full, the water will come up to your nose' and leaves with the stick.
explain.zh: 老人掉进了碗里；汤姆说“既然你在碗里了，我想你会做你的工作……如果碗满了你还不打翻，水就会淹到你的鼻子”，然后拿着玻璃棒离开了。

q.en: What surprising truth does Tom reveal at the end about the 'woman on the moon'?
q.zh: 结尾时，汤姆揭示了关于“月亮上的女人”的什么惊人真相？

options.en: She was really a man who thought he was more important than the moon bowl
options.zh: 她其实是个男人，认为自己比月亮碗重要得多

options.en: She was the princess in disguise
She was a friendly giant
She did not exist at all

explain.en: Tom tells the king: 'the woman on the moon is really a man. And he changes his mind not because he's silly, but because he thinks he's much more important than the moon bowl.'
explain.zh: 汤姆告诉国王：“月亮上的女人其实是个男人。他改变主意不是因为傻，而是因为他认为自己比月亮碗重要得多。”

q.en: What lesson is the author teaching by showing the people wrongly judged the 'woman' as silly?
q.zh: 作者通过描写人们错误地认为那个“女人”很傻，想传达什么道理？

options.en: That women are always right
That it is wrong to judge others as silly; the real problem was a proud man ignoring his duty
options.zh: 不该轻易把别人看作愚蠢；真正的问题是那个骄傲的男人忽视了自己的职责

options.en: That the moon is made of cheese
That woodchoppers should never marry princesses

explain.en: The story shows people blamed a 'silly woman' for the weather, but Tom discovers the truth: a proud old man ignored his duty. The twist teaches readers not to judge by appearances or assumptions.
explain.zh: 故事里人们把天气归咎于“傻女人”，但汤姆发现了真相：是一个骄傲的老人忽视了自己的职责。这个反转告诉读者不要凭表象或臆断去评判他人。

prompt.en: Write a new short scene where Tom returns to the moon years later and talks with the old man. Show whether the old man has changed his mind about the stars and the moon bowl.
prompt.zh: 写一段新的小场景：多年后汤姆回到月亮上，与老人交谈。写出老人对星星和月亮碗的看法是否改变了。

en: Remember the old man's proud belief that lining up stars was more important than the bowl.
Show Tom using his polite manner to teach a small lesson about doing one's duty.
zh: 记住老人骄傲地认为排列星星比月亮碗更重要。
写出汤姆用他礼貌的方式，悄悄教给对方一个关于“履行职责”的小道理。
